description_long: This object is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a combination of rough, natural surfaces and sharply cut, geometric faces. The stone features a prominent vertical slab that rises from a broader, more rugged base, giving it a striking, monolithic appearance. The natural portions of the stone are jagged and uneven, with visible textural variations and subtle color gradations from light gray to darker tones. The cut surfaces are smoother and more angular, contrasting with the organic, weathered look of the rest of the stone. The overall form suggests it could serve as a sculptural barrier or decorative feature in an urban landscape, such as a park or plaza. There are no visible openings or cavities, and the object appears solid and heavy.
description: A large, monolithic stone with both rough, natural surfaces and sharply cut geometric faces, featuring a vertical slab rising from a rugged base. The stone displays a mix of light and dark gray tones with a striking, sculptural presence.
description_view_0: Side view showing the vertical slab and the rugged, protruding base, highlighting the contrast between the smooth cut and rough natural surfaces.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the pointed, jagged top and the narrow profile of the stone, with the natural texture most prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the depth of the vertical slab and the irregular, darker base, with more shadowed areas.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tall, slender slab and the rough, rocky base, with the stone's height and verticality most apparent.
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with a mix of rough and smooth surfaces; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with some smoother, freshly cut faces; natural surfaces show signs of exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with paved or landscaped urban environments, standing out as a sculptural or functional barrier.
